element_introduction_order.mastery_context: normal game, two-minute drill, or Sega Bowl mode
select number of players for single or multiplayer
arcade-style passing mechanics, simplified playbook (13 plays total)
quarterback and receiver positioning without 3D field visualization

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: arcade-style with accelerated passing emphasis; two-minute drill mode creates artificial time compression tension

difficulty_curve.early_game: simplified ruleset and 13-play offensive scheme reduces barrier to entry for casual players
difficulty_curve.mid_game: opponent AI difficulty scales with game progression; 16 teams provide variable challenge levels
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sega Bowl mode and extended gameplay sessions increase demand for strategy and consistency
notable_spikes: two-minute drill mode introduces sudden time pressure
multiplayer competition against human players increases unpredictability

notable_level_patterns.description: Normal game → two-minute drill (time-compressed) → Sega Bowl (championship mode). Each mode escalates pressure and consequence.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Two-minute drill compresses full quarter into rapid-fire plays

notable_level_patterns.description: Deliberate reduction from 113 plays to 13 plays creates decision clarity and forces mastery through repetition rather than complexity
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Offensive drive relies on mixing limited passing routes

notable_level_patterns.description: No 3D field visualization; flat presentation emphasizes speed over simulation depth. Passing-intensive offense keeps ball in air, reducing dead time.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Every drive favors aerial attack over ground game
